metadata.description: Windows Registry Forensic Artifacts — semantically grouped by software, configuration concept, or Windows subsystem. Each entry covers one or more related registry keys. Parser references indicate tools that read this artifact. Sources: RegRipper, Autoruns, SBECmd, AmcacheParser, LOLRMM.io, MITRE ATT&CK.

categories.name: Autostart & Persistence
categories.description: Registry keys that cause code to execute at boot, logon, or via Windows subsystem hooks.

categories.name: User Activity & MRU
categories.description: User behavior artifacts — browsing history, recently accessed files, search terms, and folder navigation.

categories.name: System Information
categories.description: Core system configuration — environment variables, power settings, locale, and boot configuration.

categories.name: Security, Accounts & Policy
categories.description: Security policy configuration, local user accounts, audit settings, and authentication configuration.

categories.name: Network & Remote Access
categories.description: Network configuration, remote access tools, and network connection history.

categories.name: USB Devices & Hardware
categories.description: Hardware device connection history — USB storage, Bluetooth, portable devices, and imaging hardware.

categories.name: Software Installation & Execution
categories.description: Application installation, execution history, compatibility configuration, and OS version information.

categories.name: Execution Artifacts
categories.description: Program execution evidence beyond the Uninstall/Amcache layer — BAM, SRUM, Syscache, and configuration affecting execution artifacts.

categories.name: Applications & Communications
categories.description: Application-specific artifacts — Office document activity, Outlook profiles, media player history, and communication tools.

categories.name: Cloud & Modern Windows
categories.description: Modern Windows artifacts — UWP/AppX packages, OneDrive, and Windows Subsystem for Linux.

categories.name: Malware Indicators
categories.description: Registry artifacts associated with specific malware families, anti-forensic tools, and attacker defense evasion activity.

categories.name: Third-Party Tools & Applications
categories.description: Configuration and artifacts from third-party software — archive tools, cleanup utilities, development tools, and legacy applications.

categories.name: Other Registry Artifacts
categories.description: Registry artifacts that span multiple categories or serve specialized investigative purposes.

categories.name: lolrmm_abused_rmm_tools
categories.description: Registry artifacts for 23 Remote Monitoring & Management (RMM) tools documented in the LOLRMM project (lolrmm.io). These tools have legitimate uses but are frequently abused by ransomware operators, A

categories.name: notable_attacker_tools
categories.description: Registry artifacts for commonly abused tools with specific registry footprints: PsExec (temporary service key on target), FileZilla (FTP exfiltration), MEGASync (cloud exfiltration with autorun), Adva

categories.name: portable_tools_no_registry
categories.description: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evide
